              -- Use the fact that permutation is preserved when we can move an element to a different position
              have h16 : (min idx1 idx2) <= (max idx1 idx2) := by
                cases' le_total idx1 idx2 with h17 h17 <;> simp_all [Nat.min_eq_left, Nat.min_eq_right,
                  Nat.max_eq_left, Nat.max_eq_right] <;> omega
              have h17 : (max idx1 idx2) <= l2.length := by
                -- Prove that the maximum of the two indices is less than or equal to the length of the list
                cases' le_total idx1 idx2 with h18 h18 <;> simp_all [Nat.min_eq_left, Nat.min_eq_right,
                  Nat.max_eq_left, Nat.max_eq_right] <;> omega
              -- Use the fact that permutation is preserved when we can move an element to a different position
              by_cases h18 : min idx1 idx2 < max idx1 idx2
              <;> simp_all [List.insertIdx, Nat.min_eq_left, Nat.min_eq_right, Nat.max_eq_left, Nat.max_eq_right]
              <;> try omega
              <;> try aesop
            exact h15
          have h19 : (l2.insertIdx (max idx1 idx2) elt).Perm (l2.insertIdx idx2 elt) := by
            -- We can always insert an element at the second index
            have h20 : (max idx1 idx2) <= l2.length := by
              -- Prove that the maximum of the two indices is less than or equal to the length of the list
              cases' le_total idx1 idx2 with h21 h21 <;> simp_all [Nat.min_eq_left, Nat.min_eq_right,
                Nat.max_eq_left, Nat.max_eq_right] <;> omega
            -- Use the fact that permutation is preserved when we can move an element to a different position
            by_cases h21 : max idx1 idx2 < idx2
            <;> simp_all [List.insertIdx, Nat.min_eq_left, Nat.min_eq_right, Nat.max_eq_left, Nat.max_eq_right]
            <;> try omega
            <;> try aesop
          -- Combine the permutations to get the final result
          have h22 : (l2.insertIdx idx1 elt).Perm (l2.insertIdx (min idx1 idx2) elt) := h7
          have h23 : (l2.insertIdx (min idx1 idx2) elt).Perm (l2.insertIdx (max idx1 idx2) elt) := h14
          have h24 : (l2.insertIdx (max idx1 idx2) elt).Perm (l2.insertIdx idx2 elt) := h19
          calc
            (l2.insertIdx idx1 elt) = (l2.insertIdx idx1 elt) := rfl
            _ ≈ (l2.insertIdx (min idx1 idx2) elt) := by
              simpa [Perm] using h22
            _ ≈ (l2.insertIdx (max idx1 idx2) elt) := by
              simpa [Perm] using h23
            _ ≈ (l2.insertIdx idx2 elt) := by
              simpa [Perm] using h24
        exact h6
      exact h5
  -- Combine the two permutations to get the final result
  calc
    (l1.insertIdx idx1 elt) = (l1.insertIdx idx1 elt) := rfl
    _ ≈ (l2.insertIdx idx1 elt) := by simpa [Perm] using h1
    _ ≈ (l2.insertIdx idx2 elt) := by simpa [Perm] using h4

simp_all [List.insertIdx, List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil]
        <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] }) <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> aesop }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> omega }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> aesop }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> omega })
      · -- Case: remove
        intro idx1 idx2 hidx1 hidx2
        intro elt
        cases idx1 <;> cases idx2 <;> simp_all [List.insertIdx, List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil]
        <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] }) <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> aesop }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> omega }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> aesop }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> omega })
      <;>
        (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] }) <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> aesop }) <;>
        (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> omega }) <;>
        (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> aesop }) <;>
        (try { simp_all [List.perm_cons', List.perm_nil] <;> omega })
    -- Apply the general lemma to the specific case
    exact h hPerm
